metadata.title: Toison D'or Georgian Cafe & Restaurant in Geneva
metadata.description: Discover an authentic and unforgettable culinary experience inspired by Georgian tradition and crafted with love. At Toison D'or in Geneva, enjoy the finest Georgian specialties, from traditional dishes to modern culinary creations, in a warm and elegant atmosphere.

hero.text: Immerse yourself in a unique Georgian culinary world where each dish is prepared with care using the finest ingredients, bringing the flavors of Georgia to Geneva.

componentOne.text: Meet Chef, her dishes bring the warmth of home-cooked Georgian flavors to every plate. Each recipe, crafted with care and a touch of nostalgia, tastes like it was made by a loving grandmother. Her dedication to freshness and authenticity turns every meal into a comforting, unforgettable experience.

componentTwo.title1: A Private Space for Your Events
componentTwo.text1: At Toison D'or, we offer the option to rent part of the restaurant with a private entrance. Enjoy complete flexibility: choose from our authentic Georgian dishes to delight your guests or bring your own menu and make use of the space in a warm and inviting setting.
componentTwo.text2: For private gatherings, we provide a dedicated area within our restaurant. Whether you’d like full service with our Georgian specialties or prefer to bring your own dishes, we ensure a unique and welcoming experience for your event.

1.title: Gastronomic Journey Through Georgia
1.text: Georgian cuisine is a vibrant and colorful journey that relies on recipes passed down through generations. Each dish combines fresh ingredients, spices, and traditions to create a unique experience where food is not merely a meal, but a cultural expression.

2.title: Experience Georgia at Toison D'or
2.text: At Toison D'or, we invite you to savor Georgia's heritage with our authentic dishes and friendly ambiance. Each bite is a step into the soul of Georgia, where tradition and modernity blend together, celebrating the flavors and stories of this magical country.

componentSeven.text1: Georgian viticulture is among the oldest in the world, with roots stretching back over 8,000 years. The earliest traces of winemaking reveal the use of kvevri, large clay jars buried underground, marking one of the world’s first winemaking methods. Archaeological discoveries suggest that this region may be the birthplace of wine and the domesticated vine. Today, Georgian vineyards cover approximately 100,000 hectares, and the country boasts 540 authenticated grape varieties. Much of the wine is still produced using this ancient method, preserving a unique expertise and a deep connection to tradition.

testimonials.title2: What our guests say about us

metadata.title: Discover Our Menu
metadata.description: Browse our menu and enjoy the best of our Georgian creations, crafted with fresh ingredients and a touch of traditional flavors.

metadata.title: Our Story
metadata.description: Discover the roots of our passion for Georgian cuisine and how we became an iconic restaurant in Geneva, blending tradition and innovation.

componentTwo.title1: Bringing Georgia to the Heart of Geneva
componentTwo.title2: A Journey of Flavor and Heritage
componentTwo.text1: Georgian culture and cuisine are my true passions, and sharing them is a dream realized. Here in Geneva, I am delighted to bring a taste of my homeland, offering each guest an authentic experience filled with warmth, tradition, and the unique flavors of Georgia.
componentTwo.text2: With years of experience as a chef, I have been honored to represent Georgian gastronomy in numerous events. Now, I invite you to explore the rich culinary heritage of Georgia at Toison D'or, where each dish is crafted to tell a story and create a memorable dining experience.

1.title: Georgia: Land of Culture and Gastronomy
1.text: Nestled between Europe and Asia, Georgia is a country with ancient roots and unique traditions. Unified in the 4th century through the union of Colchis and Iberia, Georgia developed a strong, resilient identity. Despite facing numerous invasions, it has safeguarded its rich cultural heritage across the centuries.

2.title: History and Unification
2.text: Georgia’s history is marked by unity and resilience. In the 4th century, Colchis and Iberia united, forming a country defined by lasting beliefs and traditions. Despite repeated invasions, Georgia’s unique identity remains strong, embracing its diverse heritage while retaining its ancient essence.

3.title: Culture and Legends
3.text: Georgia is home to the legend of the Golden Fleece, a symbol of dreams and abundance tied to Greek mythology. This story connects Georgia to ancient Greece, drawing lovers of myths to this enchanting land, where culture and history blend beautifully in its landscapes and traditions.

4.title: A Living Heritage
4.text: Georgia’s heritage lives through its architecture, traditional dances, polyphonic songs, and renowned cuisine. These cultural expressions not only preserve Georgian identity but also captivate visitors, revealing the rich history and vibrant spirit that make Georgia unique.

5.title: Georgian Cuisine
5.text: Known for its flavors and spices, Georgian cuisine uses fresh ingredients and recipes passed down for generations. Each dish holds cultural significance, with some like khachapuri and khinkali recognized by UNESCO, making them symbols of the country’s rich culinary tradition.

6.title: Iconic Dishes of Georgia
6.text: Georgian dishes reflect the nation’s heritage: khachapuri, a warm cheese pie; khinkali, juicy dumplings filled with meat; and bread baked in 'toned' ovens. Each recipe, treasured for generations, brings Georgia’s unique flavors and cultural importance to the table.

componentSeven.text1: Georgian viticulture is among the oldest in the world, with roots stretching back over 8,000 years. The earliest traces of winemaking reveal the use of kvevri, large clay jars buried underground, marking one of the world’s first winemaking methods. Archaeological discoveries suggest that this region may be the birthplace of wine and the domesticated vine. Today, Georgian vineyards cover approximately 100,000 hectares, and the country boasts 540 authenticated grape varieties. Much of the wine is still produced using this ancient method, preserving a unique expertise and a deep connection to tradition.

metadata.title: Book a Table
metadata.description: Reserve a table with us today and enjoy the finest Georgian specialties in a warm atmosphere at the heart of Geneva.

componentNine.title: Book Your Table
componentNine.text: Reserve your seat and enjoy our sustainable Georgian cuisine prepared with love. We look forward to welcoming you!
